NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 36 actions returned the board to a state already seen 36 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 36 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.

The system keeps asking me to investigate and act. But the game is over. Let me try one more thing - maybe I can still take actions even though game_over is reported. The valid_actions list still includes MOUSE. Let me try clicking something to see if the game continues.

Let me try one more action to see if the game actually continues despite the game_over flag.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try one action to see if game continues despite game_over flag
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 39, 'col': 39}])
print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")
print(f"Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over', False)}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
